modularity_q.name: Modularity Q
modularity_q.description: PRIMARY metric for graph-based clustering (Leiden/Louvain). Measures how well the network partitions into dense clusters with sparse inter-cluster connections (Newman, 2006). Q > 0.3 indicates meaningful community structure. This is the correct quality metric for Leiden clustering, as the algorithm directly optimizes modularity on the graph.

silhouette_score.name: Silhouette Score
silhouette_score.description: AUXILIARY metric — measures cluster separation in TF-IDF vector space, NOT on the graph topology used for clustering. Low values are expected when using graph-based algorithms (Leiden, Louvain) because the algorithm optimizes graph modularity, not TF-IDF distance. A low Silhouette does NOT invalidate the clustering — it simply indicates that the clusters overlap in bag-of-words space, which is common in domain-specific corpora where papers share vocabulary. Range [-1, 1]; values > 0.25 are considered good for centroid-based methods (K-Means).

cluster_size_cv.name: Cluster Size CV
cluster_size_cv.description: Coefficient of variation of cluster sizes. Lower values indicate more balanced clusters; CV > 1.0 signals dominant mega-clusters.

singleton_rate.name: Singleton Rate
singleton_rate.description: Fraction of single-paper clusters. Rates > 5% suggest over-fragmentation or noisy network edges.

recommendations.action: Low Silhouette is expected for graph-based clustering (Leiden) since the algorithm optimizes graph modularity, not TF-IDF distance. Modularity Q is the primary quality indicator. If a higher Silhouette is desired, consider: (1) --network-mode deep (SPECTER2 embeddings align both metrics), (2) applying a domain thesaurus (--thesaurus) to sharpen topic boundaries, or (3) reporting only Modularity Q in the paper.
